segments.text: Then Balaam told Balak, {
Build seven altars for me here, and prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me to sacrifice.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Balak did as Balaam had said, and together they offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
Balaam said to Balak, {
Wait here beside your burnt offering while I go and see if perhaps the Lord will come and meet with me. Whatever he reveals to me, I'll share with you.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then Balaam left to climb up a rocky crag.
God met with him there, and Balaam said. {
I have built seven altars and on each altar I have offered a bull and a ram.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The Lord gave Balaam a message to share. He told him, {
Go back to Balak and this is what you are to say to him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So he went back to Balak, who was waiting there beside his burnt offering, along with all the Moabite leaders.
This is the declaration that Balaam gave: {
Balak brought me from Aram; the king of Moab brought me from the mountains of the east. He said, ‘Come and curse Jacob for me! Come and condemn Israel!’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But how can I curse what God has not cursed? How can I condemn what the Lord has not condemned?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I'm looking down on them from the top of rocky crags; I'm watching them from the hills. I see a people who live on their own, different from the other nations.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who can count Jacob's descendants? They are so many they're like dust! Who can count even a quarter of the Israelites? I would like to die as a good person dies! Let the end of my life be like their end!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then Balak complained to Balaam, {
What on earth have you done to me? I brought you here to curse my enemies, and now look! All you have done is bless them!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But Balaam replied, {
Don't you think I should say precisely what the Lord tells me?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Then Balak said to him, {
Please come with me to another place where you can see them. But you'll only see a part of their camp—you won't see all of them. You can curse them for me from there.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: He took him to the field of Zophim at the top of Mount Pisgah. There he built seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
Balaam told Balak, {
Wait here beside your burnt offering while I meet the Lord over there.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: The Lord met Balaam and gave him a message to share. He told him, {
Go back to Balak and this is what you are to say to him.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: So he went back to Balak, who was waiting there beside his burnt offering, along with all the Moabite leaders. {
What did the Lord say?
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Balak asked.
This is the prophecy that Balaam delivered: {
St and up, Balak, and pay attention! Listen to me, son of Zippor!
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: God isn't a human being who would lie. He's not a mere mortal who changes his mind. Does he say he's going to do something but doesn't? Does he make promises he doesn't carry out?
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Look, I have been ordered to give a blessing. God has blessed, and I can't change that.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: He's not expecting anything bad will happen to Jacob; he doesn't foresee any trouble for Israel. The Lord their God is with them; they celebrate him as their king.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: God led them out of Egypt with great power, as strong as an ox.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: No spell can be cast against Jacob; no magic can be used against Israel. People will talk about Jacob and Israel, saying, ‘What amazing things God has done for them!’
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Look! The Israelites go out hunting like a lioness; they chase like a lion. They don't rest until they eat their prey, and drink the blood of their dead victim.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Then Balak said to Balaam, {
If you can't give them any curses then at least don't give them any blessings!
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: But Balaam replied, {
Didn't I explain to you that I have to do whatever the Lord tells me?
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Please come with me and I will take you somewhere else,
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Balak said.
Maybe God would let you curse them for me from there.
segments.speaker: Balak

segments.text: Balak took Balaam to the top of Mount Peor, which looks down on the wastelands.
Balaam told Balak, {
Build seven altars for me here, and prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me to sacrifice.
segments.speaker: Balaam

segments.text: Balak did what Balaam told him, and he offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
